mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month rise in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This is a persistent, always-on trend effect independent of regime.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS rebounded sharply from pandemic trough in May-June 2020
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or positive PAYEMS momentum precedes negative returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) cross back above their pre-shock baseline level after a sharp contraction, it signals a durable consumption recovery that triggers a short-term risk-on repricing in equities over the following week, producing positive 5-day forward SPX returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS level crosses above its pre-shock (Feb 2020) peak value
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS value 485545 as of asof=t-2mo shows strong rebound from April 2020 trough
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) in the 30 trading days following RSAFS crossing its pre-shock peak, across at least 3 historical recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of near-zero FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL drives real policy rates deeply negative, which mechanically lowers the equity discount rate and incentivizes reallocation from cash to equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon. The effect requires the joint condition of low nominal rates AND positive inflation, not either alone.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% with CPIAUCSL at 257.214 as of snapshot
failure_condition: Rank IC of (CPIAUCSL YoY - FEDFUNDS) real-rate proxy against 60-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.04 over any 2-year window, or negative real rates coincide with negative forward returns in over 50% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery regime, a decline in UNRATE of more than 1 percentage point over two months signals rapid labor market healing that reduces recession-tail risk pricing, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. In non-recovery regimes, the same signal is muted or absent because it reflects mean-reversion rather than genuine recovery.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 11.1% as of asof=t-1mo, down sharply from April 2020 peak near 14.7%
failure_condition: In non-recovery regimes (e.g., late-cycle expansion), UNRATE declines of similar magnitude fail to produce distinguishable forward return patterns, or during recovery regime the hit rate of positive 20d returns following the trigger is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and forward-looking business investment, which supports higher corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon. This is a continuous, always-on supply-side trend signal.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.4587 as of asof=t-1mo, recovering from pandemic trough
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month INDPRO growth and 60-day forward SPX returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or INDPRO expansions coincide with negative returns in more than 50% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) rise above a 900k-unit threshold following a sharp contraction, it signals renewed confidence in durable-goods demand and household balance sheets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as the recovery broadens into rate-sensitive sectors.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 900 thousand units after prior sub-900k reading
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 974.0 thousand units as of asof=t-2mo, rebounding from pandemic low
failure_condition: Following HOUST crossing 900k threshold, SPX forward 20d returns are not significantly positive across at least 3 historical episodes (p>0.05), or hit rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising payrolls (PAYEMS) and rising retail sales (RSAFS) in the same month indicates a self-reinforcing income-spending cycle that produces a short-term momentum burst in equities over the following week. Neither series alone reliably predicts the short-horizon move; the interaction of labor income growth and realized spending growth is required.
evidence_refs: fact:both PAYEMS and RSAFS show synchronized month-over-month gains in the 2020 recovery snapshot
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following joint PAYEMS+RSAFS increases are not significantly different from returns following either signal alone (interaction term insignificant, p>0.10) across historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a deep-recession regime marked by a large negative GDPC1 print, a subsequent rebound in RSAFS above pre-recession trend signals that the real-economy contraction was V-shaped rather than protracted, which drives a sustained quarterly equity re-rating and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. Outside of deep-recession regimes this combination has no special predictive value.
candidate_trigger: trailing quarterly GDPC1 growth < -5% annualized followed by RSAFS exceeding pre-recession peak
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 value 18977.363 as of asof=t-6mo reflects sharp Q2 2020 contraction, while RSAFS shows strong rebound
regime_hint: deep recession followed by V-shaped recovery
failure_condition: In episodes where GDPC1 contracts sharply and RSAFS subsequently exceeds its pre-recession peak, 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (p>0.05), or the pattern fails to replicate in at least 2 of 3 historical recession-recovery episodes.
